what: Fix negative zero in formatted strings — fix_negative_zero
title: Fix negative zero in formatted strings — fix_negative_zero
text: Corrects floating-point rounding artifacts produce "-0.00" similar negative zero strings. Works character vectors, replacing patterns like "-0.00", "-0.000", etc. positive equivalents, even embedded within larger strings (e.g., "(-0.00, 1.23)" becomes "(0.00, 1.23)").

title: Fix negative zero in formatted strings — fix_negative_zero
code: fix_negative_zero(x, marks = NULL)

title: Fix negative zero in formatted strings — fix_negative_zero
text: x Character vector formatted numbers. marks Optional list big.mark decimal.mark returned resolve_number_marks. NULL, uses default US period decimal.

title: Fix negative zero in formatted strings — fix_negative_zero
text: Character vector negative zeros corrected.

title: Fix negative zero in formatted strings — fix_negative_zero
text: marks supplied, also replaces period decimal mark locale-appropriate decimal mark.

what: Determine CI separator for forest plot text annotations — forest_ci_separator
title: Determine CI separator for forest plot text annotations — forest_ci_separator
text: Returns appropriate separator string CI lower upper bounds forest plot annotations. Considers whether values may negative current locale's decimal mark.

title: Determine CI separator for forest plot text annotations — forest_ci_separator
code: forest_ci_separator(has_negatives, marks = NULL)

title: Determine CI separator for forest plot text annotations — forest_ci_separator
text: has_negatives Logical indicating whether CI bounds negative. marks Optional list big.mark decimal.mark returned resolve_number_marks.

title: Determine CI separator for forest plot text annotations — forest_ci_separator
text: Character string separator.

what: Format a categorical statistic for display — format_categorical_stat
title: Format a categorical statistic for display — format_categorical_stat
text: Converts frequency counts formatted display strings following standard conventions (n, n (%), % ) locale-aware decimal marks.

title: Format a categorical statistic for display — format_categorical_stat
code: format_categorical_stat(n, total, stat_type, marks)

title: Format a categorical statistic for display — format_categorical_stat
text: n Integer count category. total Integer total count percentage calculation. stat_type Character string: "n", "n_percent", "percent". marks List big.mark decimal.mark returned resolve_number_marks.

title: Format a categorical statistic for display — format_categorical_stat
text: Character string formatted statistic.

what: Apply formatting to column headers in exported tables (PDF/LaTeX) — format_column_headers
title: Apply formatting to column headers in exported tables (PDF/LaTeX) — format_column_headers
text: Formats column headers LaTeX output escaping special characters, italicizing 'n' 'p', optionally adding vertical spacing.

title: Apply formatting to column headers in exported tables (PDF/LaTeX) — format_column_headers
code: format_column_headers(col_names, add_header_space = TRUE)

title: Apply formatting to column headers in exported tables (PDF/LaTeX) — format_column_headers
text: col_names Character vector column names. add_header_space Logical whether add vertical padding.

title: Apply formatting to column headers in exported tables (PDF/LaTeX) — format_column_headers
text: Character vector LaTeX-formatted column names.

what: Apply formatting to column headers in exported tables (HTML) — format_column_headers_html
title: Apply formatting to column headers in exported tables (HTML) — format_column_headers_html
text: Formats column headers HTML output italicizing 'n' 'p' using HTML tags.

title: Apply formatting to column headers in exported tables (HTML) — format_column_headers_html
code: format_column_headers_html(col_names)

title: Apply formatting to column headers in exported tables (HTML) — format_column_headers_html
text: col_names Character vector column names.

title: Apply formatting to column headers in exported tables (HTML) — format_column_headers_html
text: Character vector HTML-formatted column names.

what: Format column headers with n counts (HTML) — format_column_headers_with_n_html
title: Format column headers with n counts (HTML) — format_column_headers_with_n_html
text: Creates HTML-formatted column headers sample size counts displayed column name using line breaks.

title: Format column headers with n counts (HTML) — format_column_headers_with_n_html
code: format_column_headers_with_n_html(col_names, n_row_data)

title: Format column headers with n counts (HTML) — format_column_headers_with_n_html
text: col_names Character vector column names. n_row_data Named list data.table row n values column.

title: Format column headers with n counts (HTML) — format_column_headers_with_n_html
text: Character vector HTML-formatted headers including n counts.

title: Complete Regression Analysis Workflow — fullfit
text: Executes comprehensive regression analysis pipeline combines univariable screening, automatic/manual variable selection, multivariable modeling single function call. function designed streamline complete analytical workflow initial exploration final adjusted models, publication-ready formatted output showing univariable multivariable results side--side desired.

title: Complete Regression Analysis Workflow — fullfit
code: fullfit(   data,   outcome,   predictors,   method = "screen",   multi_predictors = NULL,   p_threshold = 0.05,   columns = "both",   model_type = "glm",   family = "binomial",   random = NULL,   conf_level = 0.95,   reference_rows = TRUE,   show_n = TRUE,   show_events = TRUE,   digits = 2,   p_digits = 3,   labels = NULL,   metrics = "both",   return_type = "table",   keep_models = FALSE,   exponentiate = NULL,   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL,   number_format = NULL,   verbose = NULL,   ... )

title: Complete Regression Analysis Workflow — fullfit
text: data Data frame data.table containing analysis dataset. function automatically converts data frames data.tables efficient processing. outcome Character string specifying outcome variable name. time--event analysis, use Surv() syntax outcome variable (e.g., "Surv(os_months, os_status)"). predictors Character vector predictor variable names analyze. predictors tested univariable models. subset included multivariable model depends method parameter. method Character string specifying variable selection strategy: "screen" - Automatic selection based univariable p-value     threshold. predictors p ≤ p_threshold univariable     analysis included multivariable model [default] "" - Include predictors univariable     multivariable analyses (selection) "custom" - Manual selection. predictors univariable     analysis, specified multi_predictors     included multivariable model multi_predictors Character vector predictors include multivariable model method = "custom". Required using custom selection. Ignored methods. Default NULL. p_threshold Numeric p-value threshold automatic variable selection method = "screen". Predictors univariable p-value less threshold included multivariable model. Common values: 0.05 (strict), 0.10 (moderate), 0.20 (liberal screening). Default 0.05. Ignored methods. columns Character string specifying result columns display: "" - Show univariable multivariable results     side--side [default] "uni" - Show univariable results "multi" - Show multivariable results model_type Character string specifying regression model type: "glm" - Generalized linear model (default). Supports multiple     distributions via family parameter including logistic, Poisson,     Gamma, Gaussian, quasi-likelihood models. "lm" - Linear regression continuous outcomes normally     distributed errors. "coxph" - Cox proportional hazards model time--event     survival analysis. Requires Surv() outcome syntax. "clogit" - Conditional logistic regression matched     case-control studies. "negbin" - Negative binomial regression overdispersed count     data (requires MASS package). Estimates additional dispersion parameter     compared Poisson regression. "glmer" - Generalized linear mixed-effects model hierarchical     clustered data non-normal outcomes (requires lme4 package     random parameter). "lmer" - Linear mixed-effects model hierarchical clustered     data continuous outcomes (requires lme4 package random     parameter). "coxme" - Cox mixed-effects model clustered survival data     (requires coxme package random parameter). family GLM GLMER models, specifies error distribution link function. Can character string, family function, family object. Ignored non-GLM/GLMER models. Binary/Binomial outcomes: "binomial" binomial() - Logistic regression binary     outcomes (0/1, TRUE/FALSE). Returns odds ratios (). Default. "quasibinomial" quasibinomial() - Logistic regression     overdispersion. Use residual deviance >> residual df. binomial(link = "probit") - Probit regression (normal CDF link). binomial(link = "cloglog") - Complementary log-log link     asymmetric binary outcomes. Count outcomes: "poisson" poisson() - Poisson regression count     data. Returns rate ratios (RR). Assumes mean = variance. "quasipoisson" quasipoisson() - Poisson regression     overdispersion. Use variance > mean. Continuous outcomes: "gaussian" gaussian() - Normal/Gaussian distribution     continuous outcomes. Equivalent linear regression. gaussian(link = "log") - Log-linear model positive continuous     outcomes. Returns multiplicative effects. Positive continuous outcomes: "Gamma" Gamma() - Gamma distribution positive,     right-skewed continuous data (e.g., costs, lengths stay). Default log link. Gamma(link = "inverse") - Gamma inverse (canonical) link. Gamma(link = "identity") - Gamma identity link additive     effects positive outcomes. "inverse.gaussian" inverse.gaussian() - Inverse Gaussian     positive, highly right-skewed data. negative binomial regression (overdispersed counts), use model_type = "negbin" instead family parameter. See family additional details options. random Character string specifying random-effects formula mixed-effects models (glmer, lmer, coxme). Use standard lme4/coxme syntax, e.g., "(1|site)" random intercepts site, "(1|site/patient)" nested random effects. Required model_type mixed-effects model type unless random effects included predictors vector. Alternatively, random effects can included directly predictors vector using syntax (e.g., predictors = c("age", "sex", "(1|site)")), though screened predictors. Default NULL. conf_level Numeric confidence level confidence intervals. Must 0 1. Default 0.95 (95% CI). reference_rows Logical. TRUE, adds rows reference categories factor variables baseline values. Default TRUE. show_n Logical. TRUE, includes sample size columns. Default TRUE. show_events Logical. TRUE, includes events columns (survival logistic models). Default TRUE. digits Integer specifying decimal places effect estimates. Default 2. p_digits Integer specifying number decimal places p-values. Values smaller 10^(-p_digits) displayed "< 0.001" (p_digits = 3), "< 0.0001" (p_digits = 4), etc. Default 3. labels Named character vector list providing custom display labels variables. Names match variable names, values display labels. Default NULL. metrics Character specification statistics display: "" - Show effect estimates CI p-values [default] "effect" - Show effect estimates CI "p" - Show p-values Can also character vector: c("effect", "p") equivalent "". return_type Character string specifying return: "table" - Return formatted results table [default] "model" - Return multivariable model object "" - Return list table model keep_models Logical. TRUE, stores univariable model objects output. Can consume significant memory many predictors. Default FALSE. exponentiate Logical. Whether exponentiate coefficients. Default NULL, automatically exponentiates logistic, Poisson, Cox models, displays raw coefficients linear models. parallel Logical. TRUE (default), fits univariable models parallel using multiple CPU cores improved performance. n_cores Integer specifying number CPU cores use parallel processing. Default NULL (auto-detect: uses detectCores() - 1). Ignored parallel = FALSE. number_format Character string two-element character vector   controlling thousand decimal separators formatted output. Named   presets: "us" - Comma thousands, period decimal: 1,234.56 [default] "eu" - Period thousands, comma decimal: 1.234,56 "space" - Thin-space thousands, period decimal: 1 234.56       (SI/ISO 31-0) "none" - thousands separator: 1234.56 provide custom two-element vector c(big.mark, decimal.mark),   e.g., c("'", ".") Swiss-style: 1'234.56. NULL (default), uses   getOption("summata.number_format", "us"). Set global option   per session avoid passing argument repeatedly: verbose Logical. TRUE, displays model fitting warnings (e.g., singular fit, convergence issues). FALSE (default), routine fitting messages suppressed unexpected warnings preserved. NULL, uses getOption("summata.verbose", FALSE). ... Additional arguments passed model fitting functions (e.g., weights, subset, na.action).
code: options(summata.number_format = "eu")

title: Complete Regression Analysis Workflow — fullfit
text: Depends return_type parameter: return_type = "table" (default): data.table S3 class   "fullfit_result" containing: Variable Character. Predictor name custom label Group Character. Category level factors, empty continuous n/n_group Integer. Sample sizes (show_n = TRUE) events/events_group Integer. Event counts (show_events = TRUE) /HR/RR/Coefficient (95% CI) Character. Unadjusted effect       (columns includes "uni" metrics includes "effect") Uni p Character. Univariable p-value (columns includes       "uni" metrics includes "p") aOR/aHR/aRR/Adj. Coefficient (95% CI) Character. Adjusted effect       (columns includes "multi" metrics includes "effect") Multi p Character. Multivariable p-value (columns       includes "multi" metrics includes "p") return_type = "model": fitted multivariable model object   (glm, lm, coxph, etc.). return_type = "": list two elements: table formatted results data.table model fitted multivariable model object table includes following attributes: outcome Character. outcome variable name model_type Character. regression model type method Character. variable selection method used columns Character. columns displayed model multivariable model object (fitted) uni_results complete univariable screening results n_multi Integer. Number predictors multivariable model screened Character vector. Names predictors passed       univariable screening specified p-value threshold significant Character vector. Names variables p < 0.05       multivariable model (univariable multivariable       fitted)

title: Complete Regression Analysis Workflow — fullfit
text: Analysis Workflow: function implements complete regression analysis pipeline: Univariable screening: Fits separate models     predictor (outcome ~ predictor). predictor tested independently     understand crude associations. Variable selection: Based method parameter: "screen": Automatically selects predictors univariable         p ≤ p_threshold "": Includes predictors (selection) "custom": Uses predictors specified multi_predictors Multivariable modeling: Fits single model selected     predictors (outcome ~ predictor1 + predictor2 + ...). Estimates     adjusted variables model. Output formatting: Combines results publication-ready     table appropriate effect measures formatting. Variable Selection Strategies: "Screen" Method (method = "screen"): Uses p-value threshold automatic selection Liberal thresholds (e.g., 0.20) cast wide net avoid missing     important predictors Stricter thresholds (e.g., 0.05) focus strongly associated predictors Helps reduce overfitting multicollinearity Common exploratory analyses sample size limited "" Method (method = ""): variable selection - includes predictors Appropriate variables theoretically important Risk overfitting many predictors relative sample size Useful confirmatory analyses pre-specified models "Custom" Method (method = "custom"): Manual selection based subject matter knowledge Runs univariable analysis predictors (comparison) Includes specified predictors multivariable model Ideal theory-driven model building Allows comparison unadjusted vs adjusted effects variables Interpreting Results: columns = "" (default), tables show: Univariable columns: Crude associations, unadjusted     variables. Labeled "/HR/RR/Coefficient (95% CI)" "Uni p" Multivariable columns: Adjusted associations, accounting     predictors model. Labeled "aOR/aHR/aRR/Adj. Coefficient     (95% CI)" "Multi p" ("" = adjusted) Variables meeting selection criteria show "-" multivariable columns Comparing univariable multivariable results helps identify: Confounding: Large changes effect estimates Independent effects: Similar univariable multivariable     estimates Mediation: Attenuated effects multivariable model Suppression: Effects emerge adjustment Sample Size Considerations: Rule thumb multivariable models: Logistic regression: ≥ 10 events per predictor variable Cox regression: ≥ 10 events per predictor variable Linear regression: ≥ 10-20 observations per predictor Use screening methods reduce predictor count ratios met.

title: Complete Regression Analysis Workflow — fullfit

text: model Fitted survival model object. model_class Character string model class.

title: Extract event variable from survival model — get_event_variable
text: Character string naming event variable, NULL found.

what: Create Forest Plot for Generalized Linear Models — glmforest
title: Create Forest Plot for Generalized Linear Models — glmforest
text: Generates publication-ready forest plot combines formatted data table graphical representation effect estimates (odds ratios, risk ratios, coefficients) generalized linear model. plot integrates variable names, group levels, sample sizes, effect estimates confidence intervals, p-values, model diagnostics single comprehensive visualization designed manuscripts presentations.

title: Create Forest Plot for Generalized Linear Models — glmforest
code: glmforest(   x,   data = NULL,   title = "Generalized Linear Model",   effect_label = NULL,   digits = 2,   p_digits = 3,   conf_level = 0.95,   font_size = 1,   annot_size = 3.88,   header_size = 5.82,   title_size = 23.28,   plot_width = NULL,   plot_height = NULL,   table_width = 0.6,   show_n = TRUE,   show_events = TRUE,   indent_groups = FALSE,   condense_table = FALSE,   bold_variables = FALSE,   center_padding = 4,   zebra_stripes = TRUE,   ref_label = "reference",   labels = NULL,   color = NULL,   exponentiate = NULL,   qc_footer = TRUE,   units = "in",   number_format = NULL )

title: Create Forest Plot for Generalized Linear Models — glmforest
text: x Either fitted GLM object (class glm glmerMod), fit_result object fit(), fullfit_result object fullfit(). fit_result fullfit_result provided, model, data, labels automatically extracted. data Data frame data.table containing original data used fit model. NULL (default) x model, function attempts extract data model object. x fit_result, data extracted automatically. Providing data explicitly recommended passing model directly. title Character string specifying plot title displayed top. Default "Generalized Linear Model". Use descriptive titles like "Risk Factors Disease Outcome" publication. effect_label Character string effect measure label forest plot axis. NULL (default), automatically determined based model family link function: "Odds Ratio" logistic regression (family = binomial, link = logit), "Risk Ratio" log-link models, "Exp(Coefficient)" exponential families, "Coefficient" identity link. digits Integer specifying number decimal places effect estimates confidence intervals data table. Default 2. p_digits Integer specifying number decimal places p-values. Values smaller 10^(-p_digits) displayed "< 0.001" (p_digits = 3), "< 0.0001" (p_digits = 4), etc. Default 3. conf_level Numeric confidence level confidence intervals. Must 0 1. Default 0.95 (95% confidence intervals). CI percentage automatically displayed column headers (e.g., "90% CI" conf_level = 0.90). font_size Numeric multiplier controlling base font size text elements. Values > 1 increase fonts proportionally, values < 1 decrease . Default 1.0. Useful adjusting readability across different output sizes. annot_size Numeric value controlling relative font size data annotations (variable names, values table cells). Default 3.88. Adjust relative font_size. header_size Numeric value controlling relative font size column headers ("Variable", "Group", "n", etc.). Default 5.82. Headers typically larger annotations hierarchy. title_size Numeric value controlling relative font size main plot title. Default 23.28. title typically largest text element. plot_width Numeric value specifying intended output width specified units. Used optimizing layout text sizing. Default NULL (automatic). Recommended: 10-16 inches standard publications. plot_height Numeric value specifying intended output height specified units. Default NULL (automatic based number rows). function provides recommendations specified. table_width Numeric value 0 1 specifying proportion total plot width allocated data table (left side). forest plot occupies 1 - table_width. Default 0.6 (60% table, 40% forest). Increase longer variable names, decrease emphasize forest plot. show_n Logical. TRUE, includes column showing group-specific sample sizes categorical variables total sample size continuous variables. Default TRUE. show_events Logical. TRUE, includes column showing number events group. Relevant logistic regression (number cases) binary outcomes. Default TRUE. indent_groups Logical. TRUE, indents factor levels parent variable name, creating hierarchical visual structure. TRUE, "Group" column hidden. Default FALSE. condense_table Logical. TRUE, condenses binary categorical variables single rows showing non-reference category. Automatically sets indent_groups = TRUE. Useful tables many binary variables. Default FALSE. bold_variables Logical. TRUE, variable names displayed bold. FALSE (default), variable names displayed plain text. center_padding Numeric value specifying horizontal spacing (character units) data table forest plot. Increase separation, decrease fit content. Default 4. zebra_stripes Logical. TRUE, applies alternating gray background shading different variables (rows) improve visual grouping readability. Default TRUE. ref_label Character string display reference categories factor variables. Typically shown place effect estimates. Default "reference". Common alternatives: "ref", "1.00 (ref)". labels Named character vector list providing custom display labels variables. Names match variable names model, values labels display. Example: c(age = "Age (years)", bmi = "Body Mass Index"). Default NULL (use original variable names). color Character string specifying color effect estimate point   markers forest plot. Use hex codes R color names. Default   NULL, auto-selects based effect type: "#4BA6B6"   (teal) odds ratios (binomial/quasibinomial logit link),   "#3F87EE" (blue) rate/risk ratios (Poisson, Gamma, inverse Gaussian log link), "#5A8F5A" (green) coefficients   (Gaussian/identity link). scheme matches uniforest()   multiforest(). Choose colors contrast well black error bars. exponentiate Logical. TRUE, exponentiates coefficients display odds ratios, risk ratios, etc. FALSE, shows raw coefficients. Default NULL, automatically exponentiates logit, log, cloglog links, shows raw coefficients identity link. qc_footer Logical. TRUE, displays model quality control statistics footer (observations analyzed, model family, deviance, pseudo-R², AIC). Default TRUE. units Character string specifying units plot dimensions. Options: "" (inches), "cm" (centimeters), "mm" (millimeters). Default "". Affects interpretation plot_width plot_height. number_format Character string two-element character vector   controlling thousand decimal separators formatted output. Named   presets: "us" - Comma thousands, period decimal: 1,234.56 [default] "eu" - Period thousands, comma decimal: 1.234,56 "space" - Thin-space thousands, period decimal: 1 234.56       (SI/ISO 31-0) "none" - thousands separator: 1234.56 provide custom two-element vector c(big.mark, decimal.mark),   e.g., c("'", ".") Swiss-style: 1'234.56. NULL (default), uses   getOption("summata.number_format", "us"). Set global option   per session avoid passing argument repeatedly:
code: options(summata.number_format = "eu")

title: Create Forest Plot for Generalized Linear Models — glmforest
text: ggplot object containing complete forest plot. plot   can : Displayed directly: print(plot) Saved file: ggsave("forest.pdf", plot, width = 12, height = 8) customized ggplot2 functions returned object includes attribute "rec_dims"   accessible via attr(plot, "rec_dims"), list   containing: width Numeric. Recommended plot width specified units height Numeric. Recommended plot height specified units recommendations automatically calculated based number   variables, text sizes, layout parameters, printed console   plot_width plot_height specified.

title: Create Forest Plot for Generalized Linear Models — glmforest
text: Plot Components: forest plot consists several integrated components: Title: Centered top, describes analysis Data Table (left side): Contains columns : Variable: Predictor names (custom labels) Group: Factor levels (optional, hidden indenting) n: Sample sizes group (optional) Events: Event counts group (optional) Effect (95% CI); p-value: Formatted estimates p-values Forest Plot (right side): Graphical display : Point estimates (squares sized sample size) 95% confidence intervals (error bars) Reference line (/RR = 1 coefficient = 0) Log scale odds/risk ratios Labeled axis Model Statistics (footer): Summary : Observations analyzed (percentage total data) Model family (Binomial, Poisson, etc.) Deviance statistics Pseudo-R² (McFadden) AIC Automatic Effect Measure Selection: effect_label = NULL exponentiate = NULL, function intelligently selects appropriate effect measure: Logistic regression (family = binomial(link = "logit")):     Odds Ratios () Log-link models (link = "log"): Risk Ratios (RR)     Rate Ratios exponential families: exp(coefficient) Identity link: Raw coefficients Reference Categories: factor variables, first level (determined factor ordering alphabetically character variables) serves reference category: Displayed ref_label instead estimate confidence interval p-value shown Visually aligned categories condense_table = TRUE, reference-variables may     omitted entirely Layout Optimization: function automatically optimizes layout based content: Calculates appropriate axis ranges accommodate confidence intervals Selects meaningful tick marks log linear scales Sizes point markers proportional sample size (larger = data) Adjusts table width based variable name lengths table_width = NULL Recommends overall dimensions based number rows Visual Grouping Options: Three display modes available: Standard (indent_groups = FALSE,     condense_table = FALSE):     Separate "Variable" "Group" columns, categories shown Indented (indent_groups = TRUE,     condense_table = FALSE):     Hierarchical display groups indented variables Condensed (condense_table = TRUE):     Binary variables shown single rows, automatically indented Zebra Striping: zebra_stripes = TRUE, alternating variables (individual rows) receive light gray backgrounds. helps visually group levels factor variable together, making plot easier read especially many multi-level factors. Model Statistics Display: footer shows key diagnostic information: Observations analyzed: Total N percentage original     data (accounting missing values) Null/Residual Deviance: Model fit improvement Pseudo-R²: McFadden R² = 1 - (log L_1 / log L_2) AIC: model comparison (lower better) logistic regression, concordance (C-statistic/AUC) may also displayed available. Saving Plots: Use ggplot2::ggsave() recommended dimensions: specify custom dimensions:
code: p <- glmforest(model, data)   dims <- attr(p, "rec_dims")   ggsave("forest.pdf", p, width = dims$width, height = dims$height) ggsave("forest.png", p, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)

title: Create Forest Plot for Generalized Linear Models — glmforest
code: if (FALSE) { # Load example data data(clintrial) data(clintrial_labels)  # Example 1: Basic logistic regression forest plot model1 <- glm(os_status ~ age + sex + bmi + treatment,               data = clintrial,               family = binomial)  plot1 <- glmforest(model1, data = clintrial) print(plot1)    options(width = 180) # Example 2: With custom variable labels plot2 <- glmforest(     x = model1,     data = clintrial,     title = "Risk Factors for Mortality",     labels = clintrial_labels ) print(plot2)  # Example 3: Customize appearance plot3 <- glmforest(     x = model1,     data = clintrial,     title = "Adjusted Odds Ratios",     color = "#D62728",  # Red points     font_size = 1.2,    # Larger text     zebra_stripes = FALSE,     labels = clintrial_labels ) print(plot3)  # Example 4: Indented layout for hierarchical view plot4 <- glmforest(     x = model1,     data = clintrial,     indent_groups = TRUE,     labels = clintrial_labels ) print(plot4) # Group column hidden, levels indented under variables  # Example 5: Condensed layout for many binary variables model5 <- glm(os_status ~ age + sex + smoking + hypertension +                    diabetes + surgery,               data = clintrial,               family = binomial)  plot5 <- glmforest(     x = model5,     data = clintrial,     condense_table = TRUE,     labels = clintrial_labels ) print(plot5) # Binary variables shown in single rows  # Example 6: Hide sample size and events columns plot6 <- glmforest(     x = model1,     data = clintrial,     show_n = FALSE,     show_events = FALSE,     labels = clintrial_labels ) print(plot6)  # Example 7: Adjust table/forest proportions plot7 <- glmforest(     x = model1,     data = clintrial,     table_width = 0.7,  # More space for table     labels = clintrial_labels ) print(plot7)  # Example 8: Get and use recommended dimensions plot8 <- glmforest(model1, data = clintrial)  dims <- attr(plot8, "rec_dims") cat("Recommended: ", dims$width, "x", dims$height, "inches\n")  # Save with recommended dimensions # ggsave("forest.pdf", plot8, width = dims$width, height = dims$height)  # Example 9: Specify exact output dimensions plot9 <- glmforest(     x = model1,     data = clintrial,     plot_width = 14,     plot_height = 10,     labels = clintrial_labels ) # No dimension recommendations printed  # Example 10: Use different units (centimeters) plot10 <- glmforest(     x = model1,     data = clintrial,     plot_width = 30,  # 30 cm     plot_height = 20,  # 20 cm     units = "cm",     labels = clintrial_labels )  # Example 11: Poisson regression for count data model11 <- glm(ae_count ~ age + treatment + diabetes + surgery,                data = clintrial,                family = poisson)  plot11 <- glmforest(     x = model11,     data = clintrial,     title = "Rate Ratios for Adverse Events",     labels = clintrial_labels ) print(plot11) # Shows rate ratios with blue color for count models  # Example 12: Gamma regression for positive continuous outcomes model12 <- glm(los_days ~ age + treatment + surgery + stage,                data = clintrial,                family = Gamma(link = "log"))  plot12 <- glmforest(     x = model12,     data = clintrial,     title = "Multiplicative Effects on Length of Stay",     labels = clintrial_labels ) print(plot12) # Shows ratios with blue color (same as other ratio models)  # Example 13: Force display of raw coefficients plot13 <- glmforest(     x = model1,     data = clintrial,     exponentiate = FALSE,  # Show log odds     effect_label = "Log Odds",     labels = clintrial_labels ) print(plot13) # Reference line at 0 instead of 1  # Example 14: Custom reference label plot14 <- glmforest(     x = model1,     data = clintrial,     ref_label = "1.00 (ref)",     labels = clintrial_labels ) print(plot14)  # Example 15: Adjust font sizes for presentations plot15 <- glmforest(     x = model1,     data = clintrial,     font_size = 1.5,      # 50% larger     title_size = 30,      # Larger title     labels = clintrial_labels ) print(plot15)  # Example 16: Complete publication-ready plot final_model <- glm(     os_status ~ age + sex + bmi + smoking + hypertension +          diabetes + treatment + stage,     data = clintrial,     family = binomial )  final_plot <- glmforest(     x = final_model,     data = clintrial,     title = "Multivariable Logistic Regression: Risk Factors for Mortality",     labels = clintrial_labels,     indent_groups = TRUE,     zebra_stripes = TRUE,     color = "#4BA6B6",     font_size = 1.0,     digits = 2 )  # Save for publication dims <- attr(final_plot, "rec_dims") # ggsave("figure1.pdf", final_plot,  #        width = dims$width, height = dims$height) # ggsave("figure1.png", final_plot, #        width = dims$width, height = dims$height, dpi = 300) }

title: Create Forest Plot for Linear Models — lmforest
text: Generates publication-ready forest plot combines formatted data table graphical representation regression coefficients linear model. plot integrates variable names, group levels, sample sizes, coefficients confidence intervals, p-values, model diagnostics (R², F-statistic, AIC) single comprehensive visualization designed manuscripts presentations.

title: Create Forest Plot for Linear Models — lmforest
code: lmforest(   x,   data = NULL,   title = "Linear Model",   effect_label = "Coefficient",   digits = 2,   p_digits = 3,   conf_level = 0.95,   font_size = 1,   annot_size = 3.88,   header_size = 5.82,   title_size = 23.28,   plot_width = NULL,   plot_height = NULL,   table_width = 0.6,   show_n = TRUE,   indent_groups = FALSE,   condense_table = FALSE,   bold_variables = FALSE,   center_padding = 4,   zebra_stripes = TRUE,   ref_label = "reference",   labels = NULL,   units = "in",   color = "#5A8F5A",   qc_footer = TRUE,   number_format = NULL )

title: Create Forest Plot for Linear Models — lmforest
text: x Either fitted linear model object (class lm lmerMod), fit_result object fit(), fullfit_result object fullfit(). fit_result fullfit_result provided, model, data, labels automatically extracted. data Data frame data.table containing original data used fit model. NULL (default) x model, function attempts extract data model object. x fit_result, data extracted automatically. Providing data explicitly recommended passing model directly. title Character string specifying plot title displayed top. Default "Linear Model". effect_label Character string effect measure label forest plot axis. Default "Coefficient". digits Integer specifying number decimal places coefficients confidence intervals. Default 2. p_digits Integer specifying number decimal places p-values. Values smaller 10^(-p_digits) displayed "< 0.001" (p_digits = 3), "< 0.0001" (p_digits = 4), etc. Default 3. conf_level Numeric confidence level confidence intervals. Must 0 1. Default 0.95 (95% confidence intervals). CI percentage automatically displayed column headers (e.g., "90% CI" conf_level = 0.90). font_size Numeric multiplier controlling base font size text elements. Default 1.0. annot_size Numeric value controlling relative font size data annotations. Default 3.88. header_size Numeric value controlling relative font size column headers. Default 5.82. title_size Numeric value controlling relative font size main plot title. Default 23.28. plot_width Numeric value specifying intended output width specified units. Default NULL (automatic). plot_height Numeric value specifying intended output height specified units. Default NULL (automatic). table_width Numeric value 0 1 specifying proportion total plot width allocated data table. Default 0.6. show_n Logical. TRUE, includes column showing group-specific sample sizes. Default TRUE. indent_groups Logical. TRUE, indents factor levels parent variable name, creating hierarchical structure. "Group" column hidden TRUE. Default FALSE. condense_table Logical. TRUE, condenses binary categorical variables single rows. Automatically sets indent_groups = TRUE. Default FALSE. bold_variables Logical. TRUE, variable names displayed bold. FALSE (default), variable names displayed plain text. center_padding Numeric value specifying horizontal spacing table forest plot. Default 4. zebra_stripes Logical. TRUE, applies alternating gray background shading different variables. Default TRUE. ref_label Character string display reference categories factor variables. Default "reference". labels Named character vector providing custom display labels variables. Example: c(age = "Age (years)", height = "Height (cm)"). Default NULL. units Character string specifying units plot dimensions: "" (inches), "cm", "mm". Default "". color Character string specifying color coefficient point estimates forest plot. Default "#5A8F5A" (green). Use hex codes R color names. qc_footer Logical. TRUE, displays model quality control statistics footer (observations analyzed, R², adjusted R², F-statistic, AIC). Default TRUE. number_format Character string two-element character vector   controlling thousand decimal separators formatted output. Named   presets: "us" - Comma thousands, period decimal: 1,234.56 [default] "eu" - Period thousands, comma decimal: 1.234,56 "space" - Thin-space thousands, period decimal: 1 234.56       (SI/ISO 31-0) "none" - thousands separator: 1234.56 provide custom two-element vector c(big.mark, decimal.mark),   e.g., c("'", ".") Swiss-style: 1'234.56. NULL (default), uses   getOption("summata.number_format", "us"). Set global option   per session avoid passing argument repeatedly:
code: options(summata.number_format = "eu")
