Column name depends model type:       "(95% CI)" logistic, "HR (95% CI)" survival,       "RR (95% CI)" counts, "Coefficient (95% CI)" linear models p-value Character. Formatted p-value Wald test returned object includes following attributes accessible via attr(): raw_data data.table. Unformatted numeric results separate       columns coefficients, standard errors, confidence interval bounds,       etc. Suitable statistical analysis custom formatting models List (keep_models = TRUE). Named list fitted       model objects, predictor names list names. Access specific models       via attr(result, "models")[["predictor_name"]] outcome Character. outcome variable name used model_type Character. regression model type used model_scope Character. Always "Univariable" screening results screening_type Character. Always "univariable" identify       analysis type p_threshold Numeric. p-value threshold used significance significant Character vector. Names predictors p-value       screening threshold, suitable passing directly       downstream modeling functions

title: Univariable Screening for Multiple Predictors — uniscreen
text: Analysis Approach: function implements comprehensive univariable screening workflow: predictor predictors, fits separate model:     outcome ~ predictor Extracts coefficients, confidence intervals, p-values model Combines results single table easy comparison Formats output publication appropriate effect measures predictor tested independently - crude (unadjusted) associations account confounding interaction effects. Use Univariable Screening: Initial variable selection: Identify predictors associated     outcome building multivariable models Hypothesis generation: Explore potential associations     exploratory analyses Understanding crude associations: Report unadjusted effects     alongside adjusted estimates Variable reduction: Use p-value thresholds (e.g.,     p < 0.20) identify candidates multivariable modeling Checking multicollinearity: Compare univariable     multivariable effects identify potential collinearity Threshold p-values: p_threshold parameter controls significance threshold used printed summary count many predictors significant. predictors always included output table regardless setting. Effect Measures Model Type: Logistic regression (model_type = "glm",     family = "binomial"): Odds ratios () Cox regression (model_type = "coxph"): Hazard ratios (HR) Poisson regression (model_type = "glm",     family = "poisson"): Rate/risk ratios (RR) Negative binomial (model_type = "negbin"): Rate ratios (RR) Linear regression (model_type = "lm" GLM     identity link): Raw coefficient estimates Gamma regression (model_type = "glm",     family = "Gamma"): Multiplicative effects (default log link) Memory Considerations: keep_models = FALSE (default), fitted models discarded extracting results conserve memory. Set keep_models = TRUE following needed: Model diagnostic plots Predictions individual models Additional model statistics extracted default analysis specific models

title: Univariable Screening for Multiple Predictors — uniscreen

coxme_screen <- uniscreen(         data = clintrial,         outcome = "Surv(os_months, os_status)",         predictors = c("age", "sex", "treatment", "stage"),         model_type = "coxme",         random = "(1|site)",         labels = clintrial_labels,         parallel = FALSE     )     print(coxme_screen) } #>  #> Univariable Screening Results #> Outcome: Surv(os_months, os_status) #> Model Type: Mixed Effects Cox #> Predictors Screened: 4 #> Significant (p < 0.05): 4 #>  #>            Variable   Group      n Events      HR (95% CI) p-value #>              <char>  <char> <char> <char> what: Complete input validation for fit functions — validate_fit_inputs
title: Complete input validation for fit functions — validate_fit_inputs
text: Master validation function called fit(), uniscreen(), fullfit(). Performs comprehensive checks data structure, variable existence, numeric parameter ranges, model-outcome consistency. Returns validated parameters auto-corrections applied appropriate.

title: Complete input validation for fit functions — validate_fit_inputs
code: validate_fit_inputs(   data,   outcome,   predictors,   model_type,   family = NULL,   conf_level = 0.95,   digits = 2,   p_digits = 3,   p_threshold = NULL,   auto_correct_model = TRUE )

title: Complete input validation for fit functions — validate_fit_inputs
text: data Data frame data.table containing variables. outcome Character string outcome specification (may include Surv()). predictors Character vector predictor variable names. model_type Character string model type validate. family GLM family object, function, string applicable. conf_level Numeric confidence level (must 0 1). digits Integer number decimal places effect estimates. p_digits Integer number decimal places p-values. p_threshold Numeric p-value threshold significance highlighting. auto_correct_model Logical whether auto-correct model type mismatches.

title: Complete input validation for fit functions — validate_fit_inputs
text: List validated model_type, family, auto_corrected flag.

what: Validate model type matches outcome specification — validate_model_outcome
title: Validate model type matches outcome specification — validate_model_outcome
text: Ensures consistency specified model type, outcome variable type, GLM family (applicable). Detects common mismatches like using survival outcomes non-survival models binary outcomes linear models. Can auto-correct fixable issues raise informative errors.

title: Validate model type matches outcome specification — validate_model_outcome
code: validate_model_outcome(   outcome,   model_type,   family = NULL,   data = NULL,   auto_correct = TRUE )

title: Validate model type matches outcome specification — validate_model_outcome
text: outcome Character string outcome specification (may include Surv()). model_type Character string specified model type. family GLM family object, function, string applicable. data Data frame data.table outcome type detection. auto_correct Logical whether auto-correct fixable mismatches.

title: Validate model type matches outcome specification — validate_model_outcome
text: List model_type, family, messages, auto_corrected flag.

title: Validate model type matches outcome specification — validate_model_outcome
text: Checks mismatches auto-corrects errors appropriate.

title: Validate number_format parameter — validate_number_format
text: Checks number_format value valid use. Called early top-level functions fail fast clear error message.

title: Validate number_format parameter — validate_number_format
code: validate_number_format(number_format)

title: Validate number_format parameter — validate_number_format
text: number_format Value validate.

title: Validate number_format parameter — validate_number_format
text: Invisibly returns TRUE valid.

what: Validate outcome exists in data — validate_outcome_exists
title: Validate outcome exists in data — validate_outcome_exists
text: Checks specified outcome variable (survival variables within Surv() expression) exists dataset. Raises informative error variables missing. Handles simple outcomes Surv() expressions.

title: Validate outcome exists in data — validate_outcome_exists
code: validate_outcome_exists(data, outcome)

title: Validate outcome exists in data — validate_outcome_exists
text: data Data frame data.table check. outcome Character string outcome specification (may include Surv()).

title: Validate outcome exists in data — validate_outcome_exists
text: Invisible TRUE validation passes, otherwise stops error.

what: Validate outcome homogeneity for multifit — validate_outcome_homogeneity
title: Validate outcome homogeneity for multifit — validate_outcome_homogeneity
text: Checks whether outcomes multifit analysis compatible specified model type. Issues warning outcomes appear mixed types (e.g., binary continuous outcomes analysis), produce tables incompatible effect measures.

title: Validate outcome homogeneity for multifit — validate_outcome_homogeneity
code: validate_outcome_homogeneity(data, outcomes, model_type, family = "binomial")

title: Validate outcome homogeneity for multifit — validate_outcome_homogeneity
text: data Data.table containing analysis data. outcomes Character vector outcome variable names. model_type Character string specifying model type. family Character string specifying GLM family (glm/glmer).

title: Validate outcome homogeneity for multifit — validate_outcome_homogeneity
text: Invisible NULL. Issues warnings problems detected.

what: Validate predictors exist in data — validate_predictors_exist
title: Validate predictors exist in data — validate_predictors_exist
text: Checks specified predictor variables exist dataset. Handles interaction terms (splits ":"), mixed-effects random effects (ignores "|" syntax), raises informative errors missing variables.

title: Validate predictors exist in data — validate_predictors_exist
code: validate_predictors_exist(data, predictors)

title: Validate predictors exist in data — validate_predictors_exist
text: data Data frame data.table check. predictors Character vector predictor variable names.

title: Validate predictors exist in data — validate_predictors_exist
text: Invisible TRUE validation passes, otherwise stops error.
